skill.name: Azure.Messaging.EventGrid (.NET)
skill.description: Azure Event Grid SDK for .NET. Client library for publishing and consuming events with Azure Event Grid. Use for event-driven architectures, pub/sub messaging, CloudEvents, and EventGridEvents.
skill.summary: Publish and consume events with Azure Event Grid using .NET SDK for event-driven architectures.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Documentation-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files with risk score 0/100. All code examples demonstrate standard Azure SDK usage patterns for Event Grid topics, domains, and namespaces.

content.user_title: Build event-driven apps with Azure Event Grid .NET SDK
content.value_statement: Event-driven architectures require reliable pub/sub messaging. This skill helps you publish and consume events using Azure Event Grid with proper authentication, error handling, and best practices.
actual_capabilities: Generate code for publishing events to Event Grid topics and domains
Create EventGridEvent and CloudEvent schema implementations
Implement authentication using API keys, Azure AD, or SAS tokens
Handle pull delivery with namespace receivers and message acknowledgment
Configure Azure Functions triggers for event consumption
Implement error handling and failover patterns for production workloads
limitations: Requires Azure Event Grid resource and valid credentials to function
Documentation covers .NET SDK only, not other language SDKs
Does not configure Event Grid subscriptions or filters automatically
System event handling requires additional Azure service integrations

use_cases.title: E-commerce Order Processing
use_cases.description: Publish order events (Created, Updated, Shipped) to Event Grid for downstream processing by inventory, shipping, and analytics services.

use_cases.title: IoT Device Event Streaming
use_cases.description: Stream device telemetry and status events through Event Grid namespaces with pull delivery for reliable processing.
use_cases.target_user: IoT engineers building device management platforms

use_cases.title: Cloud-Native Application Integration
use_cases.description: Use CloudEvents standard for cross-platform event interoperability between Azure and non-Azure services.

prompt_templates.title: Publish a Single Event
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to publish a single CloudEvent to Azure Event Grid using .NET with Azure AD authentication.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: Simple event publishing with managed identity

prompt_templates.title: Batch Event Publishing
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate code to batch publish multiple EventGridEvent objects with custom JSON serialization and error handling.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: Efficient batch operations with proper error recovery

prompt_templates.title: Pull Delivery Receiver
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete pull delivery receiver that acknowledges successful events and releases failed events for retry using EventGridReceiverClient.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: Reliable message processing with acknowledgment patterns

prompt_templates.title: Azure Functions Event Trigger
prompt_templates.prompt: Build an Azure Functions v4 isolated worker that handles both EventGridEvent and CloudEvent triggers with proper logging and exception handling.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: Serverless event consumption with dual schema support

output_examples.input: Publish an Order.Created event with order details
output: Created EventGridPublisherClient with Azure AD credential
Sent event to topic endpoint successfully

output_examples.input: Receive and process events from namespace subscription
output: 1 event failed processing - released for retry
Lock tokens tracked for acknowledgment lifecycle

best_practices: Use CloudEvents schema for new implementations to ensure cross-platform interoperability
Prefer Azure AD authentication over access keys for production workloads
Implement idempotent event handlers since Event Grid may deliver events more than once
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode access keys in source code - use Azure Key Vault or managed identity
Avoid sending events larger than 1MB - split large payloads into multiple events
Do not ignore partial batch failures - handle each event acknowledgment individually

faq.question: What is the difference between EventGridEvent and CloudEvent?
faq.answer: EventGridEvent is Azure's native schema with required fields (subject, eventType, dataVersion, data). CloudEvent follows the CNCF standard with source and type fields, offering better cross-platform interoperability and extension attributes.

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure Event Grid?
faq.answer: Use Azure AD (recommended) with DefaultAzureCredential for production. API keys work for development. SAS tokens provide time-limited access. Managed Identity is preferred for Azure-hosted applications.

faq.question: What is pull delivery and when should I use it?
faq.answer: Pull delivery uses Event Grid Namespaces where receivers actively fetch events using EventGridReceiverClient. Use it when you need explicit control over message acknowledgment, retry logic, or processing throughput.

faq.question: How do I handle failed events?
faq.answer: For namespaces, use AcknowledgeAsync for success, ReleaseAsync to retry, or RejectAsync to send to dead-letter. For push delivery, configure retry policies and dead-letter destinations in your event subscriptions.

faq.question: Can I send events to multiple topics in one call?
faq.answer: For Event Grid Domains, yes - include the Topic property on each EventGridEvent for domain routing. For single topics, all events in a batch go to the same topic endpoint.

faq.question: What are the event size limits?
faq.answer: Maximum 1MB per event and 1MB total per batch. For larger payloads, store data in Azure Storage and include a reference URL in the event data field.
